description_long: This is a classic municipal fire hydrant with a robust cylindrical body painted yellow and a domed top painted white. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one facing forward, each capped with a large, round, white metal cap. The top of the hydrant is adorned with a fluted, dome-shaped cap, which includes an operating nut at its peak for valve operation. The surface shows signs of wear, with some dirt and plant debris visible near the base, and a small black label or plaque is affixed to the body. The hydrant is constructed primarily from cast metal, likely iron or steel, and is designed to be highly durable and weather-resistant for outdoor urban environments. No chains are visible connecting the caps, and there are no clear manufacturer markings discernible in the images.
description: A yellow and white metal fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, showing signs of outdoor wear. The hydrant is sturdy, cylindrical, and designed for emergency water access.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed white top, yellow body, and the forward-facing hose outlet with its cap.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back hose outlet and the domed top, with the side outlets visible.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the left hose outlet, the black label on the body, and some plant debris at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the right hose outlet and the back of the hydrant, with more visible wear and plant debris.
